blockers: Existing TL2 collapses learned row scales to one scale and exceeds the allowed relative output RMS error.
`transform_to_tl2` is data-only and recomputes a scalar scale instead of carrying checkpoint row/group scales.
The converter still derives one scalar `np.max(abs(x))` scale and writes a single sidecar scale tensor.
`GGML_TYPE_TL2` storage has no explicit row/group-scale count or dedicated row-scale TL2 qtype.
`ggml_bitnet_transform_tensor` still builds metadata for one scale, not one scale per row or row group.
Generated TL2 qgemm multiplies by `Scales[0]`; row-scale support needs kernels that index the learned output-row or row-group scale.
The x86 TL2 dispatch passes the same `wt->scales` pointer into every generated qgemm call, matching the one-scale kernel contract.
The active BitNet/Qwen TL2 loader path does not create learned row-scale sidecar tensors; TL2 expects scale metadata inside the packed tensor/kernel path.
No row-scale TL2 GGUF has passed PPL, throughput, and RSS-style evidence.

checks.blocker: Existing TL2 collapses learned row scales to one scale and exceeds the allowed relative output RMS error.
checks.name: Existing TL2 one-scale error is below product threshold

checks.blocker: `transform_to_tl2` is data-only and recomputes a scalar scale instead of carrying checkpoint row/group scales.
checks.name: TL2 converter accepts learned row/group scale metadata

checks.blocker: The converter still derives one scalar `np.max(abs(x))` scale and writes a single sidecar scale tensor.
checks.name: TL2 converter no longer recomputes one scalar max scale
All sidecar-aware TL2 converter call sites refuse row-scale sidecars safely

checks.blocker: `GGML_TYPE_TL2` storage has no explicit row/group-scale count or dedicated row-scale TL2 qtype.
checks.name: ggml TL2 storage accounts for row/group-scale sidecar

checks.blocker: `ggml_bitnet_transform_tensor` still builds metadata for one scale, not one scale per row or row group.
checks.name: TL2 transform metadata is row/group-scale aware

checks.blocker: Generated TL2 qgemm multiplies by `Scales[0]`; row-scale support needs kernels that index the learned output-row or row-group scale.
checks.name: Generated TL2 qgemm applies output-row or row-group scales

checks.blocker: The x86 TL2 dispatch passes the same `wt->scales` pointer into every generated qgemm call, matching the one-scale kernel contract.
checks.name: x86 TL2 matmul dispatch offsets scale metadata per output tile/row group

checks.blocker: The active BitNet/Qwen TL2 loader path does not create learned row-scale sidecar tensors; TL2 expects scale metadata inside the packed tensor/kernel path.
checks.name: BitNet/Qwen TL2 loader exposes learned scale sidecars when required
A compatibility-safe row-scale runtime path exists

checks.blocker: No row-scale TL2 GGUF has passed PPL, throughput, and RSS-style evidence.
checks.name: Row-scale TL2 has quality and speed benchmark evidence

required_next_steps: Add a compatibility-safe TL2 row/group-scale qtype or explicit TL2 metadata version; do not overload current single-scale TL2 silently.
Teach the converter to pass learned tensor/row/group scales into TL2 packing instead of recomputing one `max(abs(W))` scale.
Extend GGUF/ggml byte-size semantics so packed TL2 data carries the exact number of fp16/fp32 row or row-group scales.
Update generated TL2 transform metadata and kernels to index the correct scale for each output row or row group.
Update x86 TL2 matmul dispatch so each tile sees the correct scale span, and add loader-side compatibility guards for old single-scale TL2 files.
Run dense Qwen PPL, lm-eval/task quality, llama-bench throughput, and RSS benchmarks before enabling any product claim.
